Links: [{"title":"Crazy Egg API - Developer docs, APIs, SDKs, and auth. | API Tracker","url":"https://apitracker.io/a/crazy-egg"},{"title":"Crazy Egg Destination | Segment Documentation","url":"https://segment.com/docs/connections/destinations/catalog/crazy-egg/"},{"title":"Exporting from within your Reports View – Crazy Egg","url":"https://support.crazyegg.com/hc/en-us/articles/360056147194-Exporting-from-within-your-Reports-View"},{"title":"Exporting Multiple Snapshots from your Dashboard – Crazy Egg","url":"https://support.crazyegg.com/hc/en-us/articles/360056085613-Exporting-Multiple-Snapshots-from-your-Dashboard"},{"title":"Crazy Egg, Inc · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/CrazyEggInc"},{"title":"Crazy Egg | Freshpaint - Documentation","url":"https://documentation.freshpaint.io/integrations/destinations/apps/crazy-egg"},{"title":"Conversion Tracking API (Hybrid Style) – Crazy Egg Support","url":"https://support.crazyegg.com/knowledge-base/conversion-tracking-api/"},{"title":"Crazy Egg Support","url":"https://support.crazyegg.com/"},{"title":"Crazy Egg → Features, Pricing & Alternatives (2025) | ColdIQ","url":"https://coldiq.com/tools/crazy-egg"},{"title":"CrazyEgg","url":"https://getanalytics.io/plugins/crazyegg/"}]

Based on the web search results, here's what I found regarding Crazy Egg API and data export documentation:

## API and Export Capabilities

The Conversion Tracking API gives you flexible server-side goal tracking beyond standard Crazy Egg scripts. This API allows for programmatic tracking of conversions with detailed data parameters including user identifiers, URLs, values, UTM parameters, and custom data.

## Export Features

Crazy Egg provides several export options:

1. **Report Exports**: Users can click on the three horizontal dots in the top right-hand corner and select Export from within the Reports View.

2. **Multiple Snapshot Exports**: Exporting a Snapshot allows you to have an electronic copy of all or some of your reports. Exports, by default, will include all five reports or only specific reports you select.

## Integration Options

Several third-party platforms offer integration with Crazy Egg:
- Segment Documentation shows Crazy Egg as a destination for user testing with heatmaps, clickmaps and scrollmaps
- Freshpaint documentation describes Crazy Egg as a visual analytics and website optimization tool
- Analytics plugins allow integration of Crazy Egg's heat mapping, A/B testing, and session recording functionality into websites and applications

## Developer Resources

Crazy Egg, Inc has 28 repositories available on GitHub, which may contain additional tools and SDKs for developers.

For the most up-to-date and comprehensive API documentation, I recommend visiting the official Crazy Egg support site at support.crazyegg.com, particularly their Conversion Tracking API documentation which shows detailed examples of API requests and parameters for 2024-2025.

input.query: Crazy Egg REST API data warehouse ETL pipeline integration

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Crazy Egg REST API data warehouse ETL pipeline integration"

Links: [{"title":"Automate Data Ingestion: Connecting REST APIs to Your Data Warehouse with Python | by Satyam Sahu | Towards Data Engineering | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/towards-data-engineering/automate-data-ingestion-connecting-rest-apis-to-your-data-warehouse-with-python-eb889fb668f7"},{"title":"API vs ETL: What’s the Difference and Which Should You Use?","url":"https://www.domo.com/learn/article/etl-vs-api"},{"title":"ETL for API - Use Cases","url":"https://portable.io/learn/etl-api"},{"title":"How to Automate Data Pipelines with AI-ETL Tools | Integrate.io","url":"https://www.integrate.io/blog/automate-data-pipelines-with-ai-etl-tools/"},{"title":"Data Warehouse & ETL Pipelines | WebFX","url":"https://www.webfx.com/martech/services/data-warehouse-etl-pipelines/"},{"title":"REST API Integration | Integrate Data from REST API - Rivery","url":"https://rivery.io/integration/rest/"},{"title":"Best REST API ETL Tools for Seamless Data Integration - Learn | Hevo","url":"https://hevodata.com/learn/rest-api-etl-tools/"},{"title":"Top ETL Tools for Rest API Integration to follow in 2026 | Airbyte","url":"https://airbyte.com/top-etl-tools-for-sources/rest-api"},{"title":"Building an ETL Pipeline. In today’s data-driven world… | by Soorya Narayan Satheesh | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@sooryanarayan_5231/building-an-etl-pipeline-integrating-sql-csv-and-api-data-into-a-data-warehouse-321c9d26dc60"},{"title":"API Integration vs. Data Integration: Which Is Better? | Integrate.io","url":"https://www.integrate.io/blog/api-vs-data-integration/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Crazy Egg REST API data warehouse ETL pipeline integration:

## Overview

While the search didn't return specific documentation about Crazy Egg's REST API, I found comprehensive information about REST API ETL pipeline integration for data warehouses that would apply to integrating Crazy Egg data.

## REST API ETL Pipeline Integration Approach

REST API ETL tools are specialized platforms that extract, transform, and load data from RESTful APIs into target systems like data warehouses. They simplify API data integration, ensuring automation, data quality, and real-time access.

### Key Components for Integration

Build an ETL pipeline to fetch, transform, and load API data into PostgreSQL or Redshift seamlessly. The typical architecture includes:

1. **Data Extraction**: Using standard HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE, REST API ETL tools ensure consistency in data operations. You can work with multiple APIs while maintaining uniformity across systems.

2. **Data Transformation**: Once data is extracted, it's cleaned and transformed. This may include removing duplicates, correcting formats, filtering, aggregating, or enriching data. The goal is to standardize and prepare the data so it can be effectively used for analysis.

3. **Data Loading**: In the load stage, the transformed data is loaded into the target system, such as a data warehouse or data lake. This stage ensures that the data is stored in a manner that makes it easily accessible for analysis and reporting.

## Popular ETL Tools for REST API Integration

Several tools can facilitate REST API to data warehouse integration:

- **Hevo**: Hevo is a no-code data pipeline platform that simplifies data integration from public APIs to data warehouses. With its easy-to-use interface, users can effortlessly connect to REST APIs as a data source and automate data ingestion processes.

- **Rivery**: REST API Integration: ETL from REST API to Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, and more with Rivery. Start connecting the data today!

- **Integrate.io**: Integrate.io's suite of data integration tools lets you move data between locations without the hard work. For example, you can perform ETL and ELT processes with no-code/low-code connectors and reduce the amount of coding and data engineering associated with pipeline building.

## Benefits of REST API ETL Integration

Real-time data access: By directly connecting with RESTful web services, these tools ensure that data is always up to date.(no content)These tools feature built-in features for cleansing, validating, and enriching data. Businesses can access accurate, high-quality information loaded into their systems, reducing errors downstream.

## Implementation Considerations

Most API ETL platforms offer no-code or low-code setups. Non-technical users can design and manage data pipelines seamlessly across the organization.(no content)Ensure the ETL tool encrypts data, supports secure authentication, and adheres to regulations like GDPR or HIPAA. This safeguards sensitive API data and maintains customer trust.

For Crazy Egg specifically, you would need to:
1. Check if Crazy Egg provides a REST API endpoint for data extraction
2. Use one of the mentioned ETL tools or build a custom pipeline
3. Configure authentication with Crazy Egg's API
4. Set up regular data extraction schedules
5. Transform the data to match your warehouse schema
6. Load the data into your chosen data warehouse (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, etc.)

input.query: "Crazy Egg" API endpoint authentication "data export" programmatic access

content.content:
Web search results for query: ""Crazy Egg" API endpoint authentication "data export" programmatic access"

I'll search for information about Crazy Egg's API endpoint authentication and data export capabilities for programmatic access.

Links: [{"title":"Crazy Egg API - Developer docs, APIs, SDKs, and auth. | API Tracker","url":"https://apitracker.io/a/crazy-egg"},{"title":"Crazy Egg Customer-Facing Tracking API – Crazy Egg Support","url":"https://support.crazyegg.com/knowledge-base/crazy-egg-customer-facing-tracking-api/"},{"title":"Exporting from within your Reports View – Crazy Egg","url":"https://support.crazyegg.com/hc/en-us/articles/360056147194-Exporting-from-within-your-Reports-View"},{"title":"Crazy Egg FAQ | Best Website Optimization Software","url":"https://www.crazyegg.com/faq"},{"title":"Crazy Egg, Inc · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/CrazyEggInc"},{"title":"Crazy Egg Destination | Segment Documentation","url":"https://segment.com/docs/connections/destinations/catalog/crazy-egg/"},{"title":"Crazy Egg Support","url":"https://support.crazyegg.com/"},{"title":"Crazy Egg","url":"https://help.crazyegg.com/article/95-crazy-egg-api-documentation"},{"title":"Crazy Egg - Domains, IPs and App Information","url":"https://www.netify.ai/resources/applications/crazy-egg"},{"title":"Crazy Egg Privacy & Data Practice – Crazy Egg","url":"https://support.crazyegg.com/hc/en-us/articles/360054306594-Crazy-Egg-Privacy-Data-Practice"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Crazy Egg's API endpoint authentication and data export capabilities:

## Crazy Egg API Information

### API Availability and Documentation
Crazy Egg has an API to automate the process of creating and updating Heatmaps, and it provides a way for developers to view their results from within other applications. To view documentation for this API, go to the Options menu within your Crazy Egg account, and then go to the API tab.

### Customer-Facing Tracking API
Crazy Egg offers a Customer-Facing Tracking API accessible at https://track.crazyegg.com/api/v1 that uses API key authentication with the header 'Authorization: key [YOUR_API_KEY]'. This API is specifically designed for:
- Sending goal conversion events, with the ability to send multiple conversions in one request
- Tracking user conversions with a required userIdentifier field that uniquely identifies the user (could be an account ID, email address, or other identifier)

### Data Export Capabilities
Reports can be exported manually through the UI by clicking on the three horizontal dots in the top right-hand corner and selecting Export, though the search results don't indicate programmatic data export endpoints.

### Integration Options for Data Transfer
Crazy Egg offers ways to send data via native integrations including Conversion Event and Survey Notifications through email, Slack, API endpoints, or Zapier (which connects to 7,000+ apps). There's also a Google Analytics integration for A/B Testing that allows viewing traffic data segmented by test or page variant.

### Authentication Security
Crazy Egg uses TLS-encrypted POST requests to transmit authentication credentials to the Crazy Egg Service. Authentication is required for access to all application pages on the Crazy Egg Service, except for those intended to be public.

### Limitations
- The search results suggest that while there is an API for creating/updating heatmaps and tracking conversions, there doesn't appear to be comprehensive documentation publicly available for full data export APIs
- The Crazy Egg integration with Segment notes that since Crazy Egg only records data about mouse clicks and scrolls, it doesn't collect data from other standard API methods, and doesn't support mapping of user variables out of the box

To access the full API documentation, you would need to log into your Crazy Egg account and navigate to Options > API tab as mentioned in the search results.

content.content:
1→/*
     2→This view has 1 row per closed-won Hubspot deal
     3→It is useful for reporting on closed deals,
     4→as well as for joining to our MRR data to assign a deal owner to each customer
     5→(see view_latest_stripe_customer_mrr_with_hubspot_deal_owner)
     6→
     7→IMPORTANT DATA STRUCTURE CHANGE (September 2025):
     8→- HubSpot changed the structure of the closed-won field
     9→- properties_hs_is_closed_won (custom formula field) became 'super' type with all NULL values
    10→- properties.hs_is_closed_won (standard HubSpot field) remains boolean and works correctly
    11→- This likely happened due to changes in HubSpot's property hierarchy or data pipeline
    12→- Always use properties.hs_is_closed_won for closed-won logic going forward
    13→*/
    14→CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW view_closed_won_hubspot_deals AS
    15→SELECT
    16→/*
    17→Stripe customer IDs are stored in Hubspot as a custom property on the deal
    18→They are added manually, and could be NULL and/or incorrect
    19→If you see any customer IDs that are problematic or missing, send a message to Adrian and the sales team,
    20→or enter the correct value in the Hubspot deal property if you are able to do so
    21→*/
    22→hubspot_deals.properties_customer_id as stripe_customer_id,
    23→hubspot_deals.properties_dealname as dealname,
    24→hubspot_owners.firstname as deal_owner_firstname,
    25→hubspot_owners.lastname as deal_owner_lastname,
    26→hubspot_deals.properties_dealtype as deal_type,
    27→hubspot_deals.id as deal_id,
    28→hubspot_deals.properties_pipeline as deal_pipeline,
    29→hubspot_deal_pipelines.label as deal_pipeline_label,
    30→hubspot_deals.properties_amount as deal_amount,
    31→hubspot_deals.properties_createdate as deal_createdate,
    32→hubspot_deals.properties_deal_won_date as deal_won_date,
    33→-- IMPORTANT: Changed from properties_hs_is_closed_won to properties.hs_is_closed_won
    34→-- The custom formula field properties_hs_is_closed_won became 'super' type with all NULL values (Sept 2025)
    35→-- The standard HubSpot field properties.hs_is_closed_won remains boolean and works correctly
    36→hubspot_deals.properties.hs_is_closed_won as deal_is_closed_won,
    37→hubspot_deals.properties_days_to_close as deal_days_to_close,
    38→NULLIF(TRIM(hubspot_deals.properties_industry__1_), '') as deal_industry
    39→FROM public.hubspot_deals
    40→LEFT JOIN public.hubspot_owners ON hubspot_owners.id = hubspot_deals.properties_hubspot_owner_id
    41→LEFT JOIN public.hubspot_deal_pipelines ON hubspot_deal_pipelines.pipelineid = hubspot_deals.properties_pipeline
    42→WHERE hubspot_deals.properties.hs_is_closed_won = true 
    43→-- This ordering is helpful to see deals where Stripe customer ID is NULL (not yet populated in Hubspot)
    44→ORDER BY stripe_customer_id, dealname
    45→WITH NO SCHEMA BINDING;
    46→
    47→/*
    48→This view has 1 row per Stripe customer ID, with the latest MRR for each customer
    49→It is used in our MRR leaderboard (which is simply a select from this view with a limit),
    50→and is also useful for reporting on our total MRR grouped by Hubspot deal type and deal owner

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it looks malicious. If it does, you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer high-level questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
/*
This view has 1 row per closed-won Hubspot deal
It is useful for reporting on closed deals,
as well as for joining to our MRR data to assign a deal owner to each customer
(see view_latest_stripe_customer_mrr_with_hubspot_deal_owner)

IMPORTANT DATA STRUCTURE CHANGE (September 2025):
- HubSpot changed the structure of the closed-won field
- properties_hs_is_closed_won (custom formula field) became 'super' type with all NULL values
- properties.hs_is_closed_won (standard HubSpot field) remains boolean and works correctly
- This likely happened due to changes in HubSpot's property hierarchy or data pipeline
- Always use properties.hs_is_closed_won for closed-won logic going forward
*/
CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW view_closed_won_hubspot_deals AS
SELECT
/*
Stripe customer IDs are stored in Hubspot as a custom property on the deal
They are added manually, and could be NULL and/or incorrect
If you see any customer IDs that are problematic or missing, send a message to Adrian and the sales team,
or enter the correct value in the Hubspot deal property if you are able to do so
*/
hubspot_deals.properties_customer_id as stripe_customer_id,
hubspot_deals.properties_dealname as dealname,
hubspot_owners.firstname as deal_owner_firstname,
hubspot_owners.lastname as deal_owner_lastname,
hubspot_deals.properties_dealtype as deal_type,
hubspot_deals.id as deal_id,
hubspot_deals.properties_pipeline as deal_pipeline,
hubspot_deal_pipelines.label as deal_pipeline_label,
hubspot_deals.properties_amount as deal_amount,
hubspot_deals.properties_createdate as deal_createdate,
hubspot_deals.properties_deal_won_date as deal_won_date,
-- IMPORTANT: Changed from properties_hs_is_closed_won to properties.hs_is_closed_won
-- The custom formula field properties_hs_is_closed_won became 'super' type with all NULL values (Sept 2025)
-- The standard HubSpot field properties.hs_is_closed_won remains boolean and works correctly
hubspot_deals.properties.hs_is_closed_won as deal_is_closed_won,
hubspot_deals.properties_days_to_close as deal_days_to_close,
NULLIF(TRIM(hubspot_deals.properties_industry__1_), '') as deal_industry
FROM public.hubspot_deals
LEFT JOIN public.hubspot_owners ON hubspot_owners.id = hubspot_deals.properties_hubspot_owner_id
LEFT JOIN public.hubspot_deal_pipelines ON hubspot_deal_pipelines.pipelineid = hubspot_deals.properties_pipeline
WHERE hubspot_deals.properties.hs_is_closed_won = true 
-- This ordering is helpful to see deals where Stripe customer ID is NULL (not yet populated in Hubspot)
ORDER BY stripe_customer_id, dealname
WITH NO SCHEMA BINDING;

/*
This view has 1 row per Stripe customer ID, with the latest MRR for each customer
It is used in our MRR leaderboard (which is simply a select from this view with a limit),
and is also useful for reporting on our total MRR grouped by Hubspot deal type and deal owner

content.text:
## Crazy Egg Data Warehousing Assessment

Based on my research, here's the complexity assessment for warehousing Crazy Egg data in your Redshift repo:

### **Difficulty Level: MODERATE TO HIGH**

### Key Challenges:

1. **Limited API Access**
   - Crazy Egg has a Customer-Facing Tracking API for sending conversion events, but **no comprehensive data export API**
   - Full API docs are only available inside your Crazy Egg account (Options → API tab)
   - Manual exports available through UI, but not programmatic

2. **Data Access Options**
   - **Manual exports**: Reports can be exported via UI (not scalable)
   - **Webhooks/Zapier**: Can send notifications for events to endpoints
   - **No direct ETL support**: Unlike HubSpot/Stripe in your repo, Crazy Egg lacks robust data export APIs

3. **Implementation Approaches** (in order of feasibility):

   **Option A: Zapier/Webhook Pipeline** (Easiest)
   - Use Crazy Egg's Zapier integration to push events to an intermediate storage (S3)
   - Build ETL from S3 → Redshift
   - Similar to your HubSpot integration pattern

   **Option B: Browser Automation** (Complex)
   - Script automated exports using Selenium/Puppeteer
   - Schedule regular UI-based data downloads
   - Parse exported files and load to Redshift

   **Option C: Third-party ETL Tools** (Cost consideration)
   - Tools like Hevo, Rivery, or Integrate.io might have Crazy Egg connectors
   - Would need to verify current support

4. **Data Types Available**
   - Heatmaps, scrollmaps, clickmaps
   - A/B test results
   - Conversion tracking data
   - User session recordings (likely not exportable in bulk)

### Recommendation:
Start with exploring the API documentation in your Crazy Egg account and checking if Zapier integration can meet your needs. The lack of a proper data export API makes this significantly harder than your existing integrations (HubSpot, Stripe) which have robust APIs.
